title: oscore_ctx_create allows empty sender_id AND empty recipient_id
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/oscore/oscore.c  
Lines: 500-555

The oscore_ctx_create function checks that sender_id != recipient_id when both are non-empty:

```c
/*
 * Reject sender_id == recipient_id for unicast OSCORE because both peers
 * would derive identical keys. Group OSCORE (RFC 9203) may allow this,
 * but we don't support it yet.
 */
if (sender_id_len > 0 &&
    sender_id_len == recipient_id_len &&
    memcmp(sender_id, recipient_id, sender_id_len) == 0) {
    LOG_ERR("sender_id and recipient_id must differ for unicast OSCORE");
    return OSCORE_ERR_INVALID_PARAM;
}
```

However, if BOTH sender_id_len == 0 AND recipient_id_len == 0, the check passes (because sender_id_len > 0 is false). This means both IDs are empty strings, which would also result in identical key derivation.

Per RFC 8613 Section 3.2, the sender/recipient IDs are used in key derivation. If both are empty, both sender_key and recipient_key are derived with the same 'id' parameter.

Checking derive_key calls:
- Line 601: derives sender_key with ctx->sender_id, ctx->sender_id_len
- Line 611: derives recipient_key with ctx->recipient_id, ctx->recipient_id_len

If both are empty, both derive the same key, breaking the security model.

Fix:
```c
if ((sender_id_len == 0 && recipient_id_len == 0) ||
    (sender_id_len > 0 && sender_id_len == recipient_id_len &&
     memcmp(sender_id, recipient_id, sender_id_len) == 0)) {
    LOG_ERR("sender_id and recipient_id must differ");
    return OSCORE_ERR_INVALID_PARAM;
}
```

Severity: Medium - allows creation of insecure contexts with identical keys.
close_reason: Both-empty sender/recipient ID case now explicitly rejected in oscore_ctx_create (lines 570-575). Matches the suggested patch and RFC. All derive_key calls safe. Updated json export. Commit 9f25f7556
